rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks in the correct component or implementation scope
Place component type annexes in the component type scope for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Place implementation-level bindings in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections as the input AADL model
Ensure AGREE annex blocks are properly scoped within the AADL architecture
Preserve the original AADL structure and relationships when inserting AGREE annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports in implementation scopes
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants
